summary: A Flutter and Firebase-powered crime and emergency reporting platform for Ghana with offline-first reporting, evidence upload, voice-note recording, and a web-based admin dashboard.
problemStatement: Users often struggle to report incidents quickly due to poor connectivity, inaccurate location details, and limited access to formal reporting channels. The project addresses these issues with a structured, low-friction digital reporting experience.
targetUsers: Admin users managing reports and case status
Local authorities and support staff who review urgent incidents
goals: Enable fast submission of crime and emergency reports
Support offline data capture and later synchronisation
Allow admin review, filtering, and follow-up actions
Protect sensitive evidence and sensitive personal data

mobileApp: Display a home screen with Report Incident and Call 112 shortcuts
Allow users to create incident reports with category, urgency, description, location, and evidence
Save reports locally when offline and synchronise later
Show report status such as Pending, Uploading, Submitted, and Failed
adminDashboard: Allow signed-in admins to view incoming reports
Show dashboard statistics including pending, urgent, and resolved reports
Filter reports by category, urgency, status, and date
Display report details, evidence, and location on a map
Allow admins to update status, add notes, and flag spam or false reports
Provide real-time visual and audible alerts for urgent reports
backend: Use Firebase Authentication with role-based access
Apply Firestore and Storage security rules
Use Cloud Functions for media validation and orphan cleanup
Protect sensitive data and personally identifying evidence
Apply rate limiting and abuse prevention

nonFunctionalRequirements: Support poor connectivity and delayed uploads
Protect sensitive data and limit unnecessary personal information

collections.name: users
collections.purpose: Stores user and admin profile data

collections.name: reports
collections.purpose: Stores submitted crime and emergency reports

collections.name: report_media
collections.purpose: Stores metadata for uploaded evidence files

collections.name: rate_limits
collections.purpose: Tracks per-device or per-account submission limits

mvp: Voice-note reporting with local save and upload
timeline: Week 3-4: UI/UX design and project scaffolding
acceptanceCriteria: Users can install and open the app
Users can create accounts and log in
Users can submit crime and emergency reports
Reports can be saved and submitted offline
Admins can view and update reports
Unauthorised users cannot access admin data
Urgent reports trigger a visible and audible alert in the admin dashboard
constraints: Development time is limited to around 16 weeks
iOS device testing may be constrained
Google Maps and Firebase usage may require billing setup
Video and audio uploads increase storage and bandwidth usage
Poor connectivity can affect real-time submissions

risksAndMitigations.mitigation: Use offline-first local storage and background sync
Use Firebase authentication, custom claims, and Firestore rules
Use client-generated UUIDs as Firestore document IDs
Compress and limit file size where possible

successMetrics: Time taken to submit a report
Percentage of reports submitted with GPS location
Percentage of offline reports successfully synced
Number of successful voice-note reports submitted and played by admins
